questions.question: What is the main function used for in the C++ I/O System?
options: A) Opening a file
B) Closing a file
C) Reading from a file
D) Writing to a file
questions.explanation: The main function is the entry point of a C++ program and is used for the overall control flow of the program. It is not directly related to the I/O system, but it is necessary to start the program.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'ifstream' class?
options: A) To read from a file
B) To write to a file
C) To open a file
D) Both A and C
questions.explanation: The 'ifstream' class is used to read from a file. It provides methods such as 'open' to open a file, 'get' to read data, and 'close' to close a file.

questions.question: What is the 'cin' statement used for?
options: A) To write to a file
B) To read from the console
C) To write to the console
D) To open a file
questions.explanation: The 'cin' statement is used to read data from the console (keyboard input). It is a part of the C++ Standard Input/Output Streams.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'open' and 'close' methods in the 'ifstream' class?
options: A) 'open' opens a file and 'close' closes it
B) 'open' closes a file and 'close' opens it
C) 'open' opens a file in read mode and 'close' opens it in write mode
D) 'open' opens a file in write mode and 'close' opens it in read mode
questions.explanation: The 'open' method is used to open a file in read or write mode, while the 'close' method is used to close a file.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'getline' function?
options: A) To read a single character from a file
B) To read a line from a file
C) To write a single character to a file
D) To write a line to a file
questions.explanation: The 'getline' function is used to read a line from a file. It is similar to 'get' but does not stop reading until the end of the line is reached.
